checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.quote: Quarterly spike: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=592782414.48 (2010-10-02) vs SalesRevenueNet=659751327.58 (2010-07-04 to 2010-10-02) implies a one-quarter DSO jump not sustained in subsequent quarters

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: OtherAssetsNoncurrent=224880332.56 (2010-04-03) essentially flat at OtherAssetsNoncurrent=234282597.61 (2013-03-30) while revenue mix shifted, not a strong divergence

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?
Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: InventoryNet=139078142.77 (2010-04-03) grew to InventoryNet=281870984.08 (2011-04-02), +102.6%, while SalesRevenueNet=1952164045.93 (FY2010) grew to SalesRevenueNet=2522721085.83 (FY2011), only +29.2%
Inventory/Revenue ratio rose from 7.1% (FY2010) to 11.2% (FY2011) before partially normalizing to 9.3% by FY2013 (InventoryNet=214268581.26 (2013-03-30) / SalesRevenueNet=2308939067.26 (FY2013))

Gross margin remained stable/improving (GrossProfit=1524044241.34 / SalesRevenueNet=2308939067.26 = 66.0% for FY2013 vs 63.4% FY2010), so margin trend does not corroborate an inventory-quality problem

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: Quarterly net margin held in a tight 26.7%-27.6% band in FY2011 (NetIncomeLoss=168845771.41, 181949958.73, 162195726.40, 170405540.21 against SalesRevenueNet=633209705.40, 659751327.58, 603880728.47, 625879324.38) despite revenue swinging roughly 4% quarter to quarter

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=3668916.92 (2012-03-31) declined slightly to AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=3646558.46 (2013-03-30), -0.6%, while AccountsReceivableNetCurrent grew from 228870785.44 (2012-03-31) to 244000010.49 (2013-03-30), +6.6%

LiabilitiesCurrent=411808775.20 (2013-03-30) surged to LiabilitiesCurrent=1493200212.53 (2013-06-29) and LiabilitiesCurrent=1578049506.02 (2013-09-28), a ~3.8x increase in two quarters, without a matching rise in AssetsCurrent (2446270056.03 to 2506898748.51 to 2982412101.55)

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: NT 10-K filed 2009-05-28 followed almost immediately by 10-K filed 2009-06-01 and 10-K/A filed 2009-06-03

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent has only two data points (2012-03-31 and 2013-03-30), limiting multi-period reserve-adequacy analysis

Revenues tag (as opposed to SalesRevenueNet) has only three annual data points (FY2010-FY2012) filed via 0001193125-12-249215, and quarterly NetCashProvidedByUsedInFinancingActivities/InvestingActivities/OperatingActivities are populated for only a subset of quarters (mostly Q1 of each fiscal year after FY2011), limiting full quarterly cash-flow volatility comparison

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Possible reclassification of long-term debt/obligations to current liabilities, or recognition of a previously unrecorded/underaccrued obligation (e.g., litigation, put/earn-out, maturing convertible notes), producing an abrupt ~$1.1-1.2B jump in current liabilities in FY2014 Q1-Q2 without a matching increase in current or total assets, suggesting prior-period current liabilities may have been understated or the current spike reflects a previously unrecognized obligation now being reported.

rationale_evidence.quote: LiabilitiesCurrent=411808775.20 (2013-03-30) rising to LiabilitiesCurrent=1493200212.53 (2013-06-29) and LiabilitiesCurrent=1578049506.02 (2013-09-28)
AssetsCurrent only rose from 2446270056.03 (2013-03-30) to 2982412101.55 (2013-09-28), far short of the ~1.17B liability increase

StockholdersEquity fell from 3154987477.13 (2013-03-30) to 2903403689.73 (2013-06-29) despite positive NetIncomeLoss=167180598.44 for the quarter, indicating a large offsetting equity reduction concurrent with the liability spike
rationale_evidence.location: 10-Q balance sheet / statement of stockholders equity

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Rapid inventory build in FY2011 (inventory more than doubling while revenue grew only ~29%) raises the possibility that inventory reserves for excess/obsolete stock were insufficiently increased, potentially overstating InventoryNet and understating COGS/reserve expense in that period, with partial correction (write-down or reduced purchasing) evident in the subsequent decline in inventory levels in FY2012-2013.

rationale_evidence.quote: InventoryNet=139078142.77 (2010-04-03) to InventoryNet=281870984.08 (2011-04-02), +102.6%, vs SalesRevenueNet growth of +29.2% over the same period (1952164045.93 to 2522721085.83)
InventoryNet declined to 218118495.25 (2012-03-31) and 214268581.26 (2013-03-30) as revenue also declined, consistent with a partial unwind of the earlier buildup

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Allowance for doubtful accounts was held roughly flat (slightly down) while gross receivables grew, which could understate bad-debt reserve expense and modestly overstate net receivables and net income; magnitude is small but the trend runs counter to receivables growth.

rationale_evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=3668916.92 (2012-03-31) vs AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=3646558.46 (2013-03-30), a slight decline, while AccountsReceivableNetCurrent grew from 228870785.44 to 244000010.49 over the same period

top_signals: Abrupt ~3.8x spike in LiabilitiesCurrent (411.8M to 1,578.0M) across FY2014 Q1-Q2 without matching growth in current/total assets, unexplained by data provided
Inventory more than doubled in FY2011 (+102.6%) while revenue grew only 29.2%, indicating a build-up disproportionate to sales activity
Allowance for doubtful accounts essentially flat/declining while receivables grew, a mild reserve-adequacy concern
Recurring multi-year SEC CORRESP/UPLOAD correspondence cycles (2006-2013) suggesting sustained SEC staff review of filings
Goodwill grew nearly 2x the rate of revenue over FY2010-FY2013, partly acquisition-driven but adding to soft-asset growth
